result:
Alright, I've read everything. This task doesn't decompose into departments — it's asking me (the orchestrator) to do the strategic analysis. Spinning up agents to "read and summarize" would just burn tokens telling you what I can tell you directly. Here's the honest assessment:

---

## Strategy Review: 11 Runs, $12.27 Spent

### What the audits told us
The product is **technically solid**. 48/48 smoke tests pass, no SQL injection, secrets clean, accessibility clean, XSS handled. We fixed some hardcoded colors and a slug mismatch. The audits were worth doing once — but the site isn't broken. The problem isn't quality. The problem is **nobody's using it**.

**Current state: 55 users, 0 paying, unknown MCP installs.**

### Top 3 Highest-Impact Actions

**1. Get the MCP server into AI IDE directories (Distribution)**
This is the #1 blocker. The MCP server is on PyPI but there's no discovery path. Agents won't search for `indiestack` unless something tells them to. The concrete actions:
- Get listed on [Anthropic's MCP server directory](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers) — this is where Claude Desktop/Code users discover MCP servers
- Get listed on Cursor's MCP registry, Windsurf's, Cline's, etc.
- Submit to awesome-mcp-servers lists on GitHub
- Every one of these listings is free and each one could drive 50-200 installs

This is **Ed + Patrick work**, not code work. No departments needed — just submissions and follow-ups.

**2. Backfill rich metadata for top 100 tools (Data Quality)**  
The MCP audits revealed the real product gap: when an agent queries "auth" and gets a result back with no `install_command`, no `env_vars`, no `sdk_packages` — the agent can't do anything useful with it. The agent moves on and never comes back. This is already on the sprint TODO but hasn't been done yet.

The 100 most-searched tools need: install commands, env vars, SDK packages, and at minimum 1-2 verified combos each. This is a **backend/data** task — probably a script that pulls from npm/GitHub READMEs + manual curation. ~1-2 sessions of focused work.

**3. Build one compelling tool-maker outreach email with real data (Sales)**
The revenue model says $299/mo tool maker dashboards. But there's no sales collateral and no outreach has happened. The concrete action:
- Pick 5 tools where we have real migration data (e.g., "X developers migrated from Jest to Vitest based on 265 migration paths")
- Write ONE email template showing a tool maker what their competitive landscape looks like
- Send it to those 5 tool makers personally
- See if anyone bites

This tests the revenue hypothesis with <2 hours of work. If zero tool makers care, the $299/mo dashboard plan needs rethinking before building it.

---

### What NOT to do
- More audits (you said it, and you're right)
- More pages/features (18 pages is already too many for 55 users)
- More data enrichment beyond top 100 (diminishing returns)
- Building the $299 dashboard before validating anyone would pay for it

### The honest uncomfortable bit
Every orchestrator run so far has been inward-facing — checking our own pages, auditing our own code. Zero runs have been about **getting the product in front of people**. The product is good enough. The bottleneck is distribution and sales, neither of which require code.
